title: 2 Million Bees Escaped a Texas Semi. It Was Chaos.
script: 2 million bees just escaped! Sunday morning in Mauriceville, Texas, a semi hauling 408 beehives took a turn too wide and tipped over — dumping an estimated 2 million bees onto Colony Drive. The driver walked away fine. The bees did not stay in the truck. Orange County Emergency Services ordered the entire neighborhood indoors while local beekeepers in full protective suits descended on the scene. The truck had been carrying 50,000 pounds of hive boxes. When it went over, the swarm was so thick responders could barely see through it. Beekeepers spent hours loading surviving hives onto replacement trucks, standing waist-deep in bees the whole time. Every recovered colony eventually made it to a local honey farm. The town of Mauriceville is going to need a moment. With 2 million bees on Colony Drive — would you have gone outside?
